hero.title: Empowering Communities Through Action
hero.subtitle: Resti Kiryandongo CBO is dedicated to improving lives through education, healthcare, and community development initiatives in Kiryandongo District, Uganda.

about.intro: Founded with a mission to empower and uplift communities in Kiryandongo District, we are a community-based organization dedicated to creating sustainable positive change through collaborative action and locally-driven solutions.
story: Resti Kiryandongo CBO was born from a shared vision among community members who recognized the need for organized, sustainable development initiatives in our district. What started as small-scale educational support has grown into a comprehensive community development organization.

Today, we work closely with local government, international partners, and most importantly, the communities we serve, to identify needs, develop solutions, and implement programs that create lasting positive change. Our grassroots approach ensures that every initiative is community-driven and culturally appropriate.
about.title: About Resti Kiryandongo CBO

values.title: Compassion
values.description: We approach every initiative with empathy and understanding for community needs.

values.title: Community
values.description: Working together with local leaders and residents to create lasting change.

values.title: Impact
values.description: Focused on measurable outcomes that improve quality of life.

values.title: Excellence
values.description: Committed to delivering high-quality programs and services.

about.vision: A thriving, self-sustaining community where every individual has access to quality education, healthcare, and opportunities for economic prosperity.
about.mission: To empower communities in Kiryandongo through sustainable development programs in education, healthcare, and economic empowerment, fostering self-reliance and improved quality of life for all.

legal.refundPolicy:
Refund Policy

for Resti CBO

Effective Date: May 17, 2026
Last Updated: May 17, 2026

1. Introduction

This Refund Policy outlines the terms and conditions governing refunds, cancellations, and reimbursement requests related to donations, payments, services, events, programs, and other financial transactions made through the website or services of Resti CBO (“Resti CBO,” “we,” “our,” or “us”).

By making a payment, donation, registration, or financial transaction through our website or associated platforms, you acknowledge and agree to the terms of this Refund Policy.

2. Donations
2.1 Voluntary Donations

All donations made to Resti CBO are voluntary contributions intended to support our community programs, activities, operations, and charitable initiatives.

Unless otherwise stated, donations are generally considered final and non-refundable.

2.2 Refund Requests for Donations

Refund requests may be considered under limited circumstances, including:

Duplicate transactions;
Incorrect donation amounts caused by technical error;
Unauthorized or fraudulent transactions;
Accidental donations made in error.

Refund requests must be submitted within 14 days of the transaction date.

2.3 Refund Review Process

All refund requests are reviewed on a case-by-case basis.

To request a refund, users may be required to provide:

Full name;
Transaction reference number;
Date of payment;
Proof of payment;
Reason for the refund request.

Resti CBO reserves the right to approve or deny refund requests at its discretion, subject to applicable laws and payment provider policies.

3. Event Registrations and Program Fees
3.1 Paid Events or Programs

Where payments are made for events, workshops, training sessions, conferences, or programs, refund eligibility may depend on:

Timing of cancellation;
Administrative costs already incurred;
Event preparation expenses;
Participation status.
3.2 Cancellation by Participant

Refunds for cancellations initiated by participants may follow these guidelines unless otherwise stated:

Cancellation more than 14 days before the event: eligible for partial or full refund;
Cancellation within 7–14 days before the event: partial refund may apply;
Cancellation less than 7 days before the event: refund may not be available.

Administrative or processing fees may be deducted where applicable.

3.3 Cancellation by Resti CBO

If Resti CBO cancels an event, training, or program, affected participants may be eligible for:

Full refund;
Credit toward a future event;
Rescheduling options.

Refund timelines may vary depending on payment providers and banking institutions.

4. Membership Fees and Service Payments

Where applicable, membership fees, subscription payments, or service charges may be non-refundable after services have been accessed, activated, or delivered.

Exceptions may apply where required by law.

5. Digital Products and Downloadable Materials

Unless otherwise stated, payments for digital products, downloadable documents, educational materials, media content, or online resources are generally non-refundable after access or download has been granted.

6. Fraudulent or Unauthorized Transactions

If you believe a transaction was made fraudulently or without authorization, please contact us immediately.

We may investigate such transactions and cooperate with financial institutions, payment processors, or legal authorities where necessary.

Resti CBO reserves the right to temporarily suspend transactions during investigation.

7. Chargebacks and Payment Disputes

Users are encouraged to contact Resti CBO directly before initiating a chargeback or payment dispute through their bank or payment provider.

Improper or fraudulent chargebacks may result in:

Suspension of services;
Restriction of future transactions;
Additional administrative review.
8. Processing Time for Refunds

Approved refunds may take between 7 to 30 business days to process depending on:

Payment method;
Banking institution;
Payment gateway processing timelines;
International transaction procedures.

Resti CBO is not responsible for delays caused by third-party financial institutions or payment providers.

9. Non-Refundable Situations

Refunds may not be granted in situations including, but not limited to:

Change of mind after donation or payment;
Failure to attend an event without cancellation notice;
Violation of organizational policies;
Services already delivered or completed;
Incorrect information provided by the user;
Delays caused by third-party service providers beyond our control.
10. Currency and Transaction Fees

Refunds may be issued in the original transaction currency where possible.

Users acknowledge that:

Currency exchange differences may occur;
Bank or payment provider fees may not be refundable;
International transfer charges may apply.
11. Modification of This Policy

Resti CBO reserves the right to modify or update this Refund Policy at any time without prior notice.

Updated versions will be posted on this page with a revised effective date.

12. Contact Information

For refund requests, billing concerns, or payment-related inquiries, please contact:

Resti CBO

Email: [Insert Official Email Address]
Phone: [Insert Official Phone Number]
Address: [Insert Physical Address]
Website: [Insert Website URL]

13. Acceptance of Policy

By making a donation, payment, registration, or financial transaction with Resti CBO, users acknowledge that they have read, understood, and agreed to this Refund Policy.
legal.privacyPolicy:
Privacy Policy

for Resti CBO

Effective Date: May 17, 2026
Last Updated: May 17, 2026

1. Introduction

Welcome to the website of Resti CBO (“Resti CBO,” “we,” “our,” or “us”). We are committed to protecting the privacy, confidentiality, and security of personal information collected from users, visitors, volunteers, donors, beneficiaries, partners, and all individuals who interact with our website and services.

This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, disclose, process, and safeguard your information when you access or use our website, services, programs, applications, communication platforms, and related digital services.

By accessing or using our website, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agreed to the terms of this Privacy Policy.

2. Scope of This Privacy Policy

This Privacy Policy applies to:

Visitors accessing our website
Individuals contacting us through forms or email
Donors and sponsors
Volunteers and staff applicants
Beneficiaries of our programs
Members and partners
Users subscribing to newsletters or updates
Individuals interacting with our social media or online services

This policy applies to all data collected online through our website and digital platforms.

3. Information We Collect

We may collect both personal and non-personal information from users.

3.1 Personal Information

Personal information refers to information that identifies or can reasonably identify an individual. This may include:

Full name
Email address
Telephone number
Postal or physical address
Organization or institution name
Donation details
Volunteer application information
Identification documents where necessary
Photographs or media submissions
Messages, inquiries, or feedback submitted to us
3.2 Non-Personal Information

We may automatically collect non-identifiable information such as:

Browser type and version
Device information
Operating system
Internet Protocol (IP) address
Geographic location data
Website usage statistics
Pages visited and time spent on pages
Referral links and navigation behavior
4. Methods of Information Collection

We collect information through various methods, including:

4.1 Direct Collection

Information provided voluntarily when users:

Fill out forms
Subscribe to newsletters
Register for events
Apply for volunteering opportunities
Submit donations
Contact us through email or contact forms
4.2 Automated Technologies

We may use technologies such as:

Cookies
Analytics tools
Tracking pixels
Server logs

These technologies help us improve website functionality and user experience.

5. Purpose of Data Collection

We collect and process information for legitimate organizational purposes, including:

Delivering community services and programs
Responding to inquiries and requests
Processing donations and sponsorships
Managing volunteer and partnership applications
Sending newsletters and updates
Improving website performance and accessibility
Conducting research and analysis
Monitoring website security
Preventing fraud, abuse, or unauthorized access
Complying with legal and regulatory obligations
6. Legal Basis for Processing Information

Where applicable under privacy laws, we process personal data based on:

User consent
Contractual necessity
Legal obligations
Legitimate organizational interests
Protection of vital interests
7. Cookies Policy
7.1 What Are Cookies

Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help websites function efficiently and improve user experience.

7.2 Types of Cookies We Use

We may use:

Essential Cookies

Required for website functionality and security.

Performance and Analytics Cookies

Used to understand website traffic and user behavior.

Functional Cookies

Used to remember user preferences and settings.

Third-Party Cookies

Used by integrated services such as analytics providers or embedded content.

7.3 Managing Cookies

Users may disable or manage cookies through browser settings. However, disabling cookies may affect website functionality.

8. Sharing and Disclosure of Information

We do not sell, rent, trade, or commercially distribute personal information.

We may disclose information under the following circumstances:

8.1 Service Providers

We may share information with trusted third-party providers assisting with:

Website hosting
Payment processing
Email communication
Data storage
Technical support

These providers are required to maintain confidentiality and security.

8.2 Legal Compliance

We may disclose information when required by:

Applicable law
Court order
Government request
Regulatory authority
8.3 Protection of Rights

We may disclose information to:

Protect organizational rights
Investigate fraud or abuse
Enforce website policies
Protect user safety
9. Donations and Financial Information

When users make donations through our website, financial transactions may be processed through secure third-party payment gateways.

We do not intentionally store sensitive financial information such as:

Full credit card numbers
Banking PINs
CVV/security codes

Users should review the privacy policies of third-party payment providers used on the website.

10. Data Security Measures

We implement reasonable technical, administrative, and organizational safeguards to protect personal information against:

Unauthorized access
Disclosure
Loss
Misuse
Alteration
Destruction

Security measures may include:

Encrypted communications (SSL/HTTPS)
Secure hosting environments
Password protection
Access controls
Regular monitoring and updates

Despite our efforts, no electronic system is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

11. International Data Transfers

If users access our website from outside the country where our servers are located, information may be transferred and processed across international borders in accordance with applicable data protection laws.

12. Retention of Personal Information

We retain personal information only for as long as necessary to:

Fulfill the purposes outlined in this policy
Meet legal and regulatory obligations
Resolve disputes
Enforce agreements

After retention periods expire, information may be securely deleted or anonymized.

13. Third-Party Websites and External Links

Our website may contain links to external websites, social media platforms, or partner organizations.

We are not responsible for:

The privacy practices of third parties
External website content
Third-party data collection activities

Users are encouraged to review the privacy policies of external websites before sharing information.

14. Children's Privacy

Protecting children’s privacy is important to us.

Our website is not intended to knowingly collect personal information from children under the age of 13 without parental or guardian consent.

If we become aware that information from a child has been collected improperly, we will take reasonable steps to delete it promptly.

15. User Rights and Choices

Depending on applicable laws and regulations, users may have rights including:

The right to access personal information
The right to correct inaccurate information
The right to request deletion
The right to withdraw consent
The right to restrict processing
The right to object to data processing
The right to data portability

Requests regarding personal information may be submitted using the contact information provided below.

16. Email Communications and Marketing

Users who subscribe to newsletters or updates may receive:

Program announcements
Community updates
Event notifications
Campaign information
Fundraising communications

Users may unsubscribe from communications at any time using the unsubscribe link or by contacting us directly.

17. Intellectual Property and Media Usage

Images, videos, logos, documents, and website content belonging to Resti CBO are protected by applicable intellectual property laws unless otherwise stated.

Users may not reproduce or distribute organizational materials without prior permission.

18. Changes to This Privacy Policy

We reserve the right to update or modify this Privacy Policy at any time.

Changes become effective immediately upon posting on this page unless otherwise stated.

Users are encouraged to review this policy periodically to stay informed about how we protect information.

19. Contact Information

For questions, concerns, complaints, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or personal data handling practices, please contact:

Resti CBO

Email: [Insert Official Email Address]
Phone: [Insert Official Phone Number]
Address: [Insert Physical Address]
Website: [Insert Website URL]

20. Consent

By accessing or using the Resti CBO website, users acknowledge and agree to the terms of this Privacy Policy and consent to the collection, use, and disclosure of information as described herein.
legal.termsOfService:
Terms of Service

for Resti CBO

Effective Date: May 17, 2026
Last Updated: May 17, 2026

1. Introduction

Welcome to the website of Resti CBO (“Resti CBO,” “we,” “our,” or “us”).

These Terms of Service (“Terms”) govern your access to and use of our website, digital platforms, services, programs, content, and any related features operated by Resti CBO.

By accessing, browsing, using, or interacting with our website or services, you agree to comply with and be legally bound by these Terms. If you do not agree with these Terms, you must discontinue use of the website immediately.

2. Definitions

For purposes of these Terms:

“Website” refers to the official Resti CBO website and all associated web pages and services.
“User”, “you”, or “your” refers to any person accessing or using the website.
“Services” refers to all programs, information, content, resources, donations, volunteer opportunities, and digital services provided by Resti CBO.
“Content” includes text, graphics, logos, videos, images, documents, publications, and other materials available on the website.
3. Eligibility

By using this website, you represent and warrant that:

You are at least 18 years old or using the website under supervision of a parent or guardian;
You have legal capacity to enter into these Terms;
Your use of the website complies with applicable laws and regulations.
4. Acceptance of Terms

Your continued access to and use of the website constitutes acceptance of these Terms and any future modifications.

We reserve the right to modify, update, or replace these Terms at any time without prior notice. Updated versions will be posted on this page with a revised effective date.

5. Use of the Website

Users agree to use the website only for lawful purposes and in a manner consistent with these Terms.

You agree not to:

Use the website for illegal or fraudulent purposes;
Interfere with website security or operations;
Attempt unauthorized access to systems or accounts;
Upload malicious software, viruses, or harmful code;
Use automated systems to scrape or collect data;
Impersonate another person or organization;
Post false, misleading, defamatory, abusive, or harmful content;
Violate intellectual property rights;
Disrupt website functionality or network infrastructure.
6. User Accounts

Certain services or features may require account registration.

Users are responsible for:

Providing accurate information;
Maintaining confidentiality of login credentials;
All activities conducted under their accounts;
Promptly notifying us of unauthorized account access.

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts violating these Terms.

7. Donations and Payments
7.1 Donations

The website may allow users to make voluntary donations to support programs and activities of Resti CBO.

By making a donation, you acknowledge that:

Donations are voluntary;
Information provided must be accurate;
You are authorized to use the selected payment method.
7.2 Payment Processing

Payments may be processed through secure third-party payment providers. Resti CBO does not store sensitive financial information unless explicitly stated.

7.3 Refund Policy

Donations may be non-refundable except where required by law or approved at our discretion.

Users may contact us regarding donation concerns or payment disputes.

8. Volunteer and Program Participation

Users applying for volunteer opportunities or programs agree that:

Information submitted is truthful and accurate;
Participation may be subject to screening or approval;
Resti CBO may accept or decline applications at its discretion.

Participation in programs may require compliance with additional guidelines, policies, or agreements.

9. Intellectual Property Rights

All website content, unless otherwise stated, is owned or licensed by Resti CBO and protected under applicable copyright, trademark, and intellectual property laws.

This includes:

Logos
Branding
Text content
Images
Videos
Publications
Graphics
Software and website design

Users may not:

Copy or reproduce materials without permission;
Distribute content commercially;
Modify or create derivative works;
Use organizational branding without authorization.

Limited personal and non-commercial use is permitted.

10. User-Generated Content

Where users submit content such as comments, testimonials, media, or feedback, users grant Resti CBO a non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide license to use, reproduce, publish, and display such content for organizational purposes.

Users remain responsible for content they submit and agree not to upload unlawful or infringing material.

We reserve the right to remove content that violates these Terms.

11. Privacy

Your use of the website is also governed by our Privacy Policy, which explains how personal information is collected, processed, and protected.

By using the website, you consent to our data practices as described in the Privacy Policy.

12. Third-Party Links and Services

The website may contain links to third-party websites, applications, or services for user convenience.

Resti CBO does not control or endorse third-party platforms and is not responsible for:

External content;
Third-party privacy practices;
Accuracy of information;
Availability of external services.

Users access third-party services at their own risk.

13. Website Availability

We strive to maintain website availability and functionality but do not guarantee uninterrupted access.

We reserve the right to:

Modify or discontinue services;
Restrict access;
Perform maintenance;
Remove content;
Update website functionality without notice.
14. Disclaimer of Warranties

The website and services are provided on an “AS IS” and “AS AVAILABLE” basis without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied.

To the fullest extent permitted by law, Resti CBO disclaims warranties including:

Merchantability;
Fitness for a particular purpose;
Non-infringement;
Accuracy or reliability of content;
Continuous availability;
Security from cyber threats.

We do not guarantee that the website will be error-free or free from harmful components.

15. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Resti CBO and its officers, staff, volunteers, affiliates, and partners shall not be liable for any:

Direct damages;
Indirect damages;
Incidental damages;
Consequential damages;
Loss of data;
Loss of profits;
Service interruptions;
Security breaches;
Damages arising from website use or inability to use the website.

Users access and use the website at their own risk.

16. Indemnification

Users agree to indemnify and hold harmless Resti CBO, its staff, officers, volunteers, affiliates, and partners from claims, damages, liabilities, expenses, or losses arising from:

Violation of these Terms;
Misuse of the website;
Violation of laws or third-party rights.
17. Termination

We reserve the right to suspend or terminate user access to the website or services at our discretion, without prior notice, if users violate these Terms or applicable laws.

Termination does not affect rights or obligations accrued prior to termination.

18. Governing Law

These Terms shall be governed and interpreted in accordance with the laws applicable in the jurisdiction where Resti CBO operates, without regard to conflict of law principles.

Any disputes arising under these Terms shall be subject to the jurisdiction of competent courts.

19. Severability

If any provision of these Terms is found invalid or unenforceable, remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

20. Entire Agreement

These Terms constitute the entire agreement between users and Resti CBO regarding website use and supersede prior agreements or understandings relating to the website.

21. Contact Information

For questions regarding these Terms of Service, please contact:

Resti CBO

Email: [Insert Official Email Address]
Phone: [Insert Official Phone Number]
Address: [Insert Physical Address]
Website: [Insert Website URL]

22. Acceptance

By accessing or using the Resti CBO website, you confirm that you have read, understood, and agreed to these Terms of Service.
